pr.title: Add optional Codex-in-container launch mode for worktree sessions
pr.body:
This change adds a containerized Codex workflow to the existing worktree launcher: create/reuse a dedicated Git worktree, then optionally run Codex inside Docker with only that worktree mounted. It keeps the default local CLI behavior unchanged for Copilot/Claude and non-container Codex.

- **Script interface updates**
  - Added `-CodexInContainer` (switch) and `-CodexContainerImage <image>` (default: `my-codex-image`) to `new-cli-worktree.ps1`.
  - Added argument guard so container mode is valid only with `-Cli codex`.

- **Containerized Codex execution path**
  - When `-Cli codex -CodexInContainer` is used, the script now runs:
    - `docker run --rm -it`
    - `--workdir /workspace`
    - bind mount of the generated worktree to `/workspace`
    - named volume `codex-home` mounted to `/home/codex`
    - `HOME=/home/codex`
  - If available, the script resolves host `uid:gid` via `id` and passes `--user uid:gid`.
  - For API auth parity, forwards `OPENAI_API_KEY` when present.

- **Docker image definition**
  - Added a repository `Dockerfile` that builds `my-codex-image`:
    - Base image: `node:22-bookworm`
    - Installs: `git`, `ripgrep`, `ca-certificates`, `openssh-client`
    - Installs Codex CLI globally: `npm install -g @openai/codex`
    - Sets `WORKDIR /workspace` and default command `codex`

- **Documentation**
  - Updated README usage and parameter docs for the new flags.
  - Added a Codex-in-Docker example aligned with the worktree-first workflow.
  - Added Docker build instructions for the included `Dockerfile`:

```bash
docker build -t my-codex-image .
```

```powershell
.\new-cli-worktree.ps1 -Cli codex -CodexInContainer -CodexContainerImage my-codex-image
```
pr.author: Copilot
